violations.text: The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. PIC knew that prep unit was not holding proper ambient temperature and continued usage of unit. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

The kitchen prep unit was observed held at an ambient temperature of 56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were evaluated and voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. Establishment shall not store TCS food items within the unit until an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less is maintained. Unit was taken out of service during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

The kitchen prep unit located was observed unable to maintain an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less and not in good repair. Establishment is to repair or replace unit. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. PIC knew that prep unit was not holding proper ambient temperature and continued usage of unit. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025
The hand-washing sink in the upstairs coffee area was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were provided during the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The kitchen prep unit was observed held at an ambient temperature of 53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were evaluated and voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. Establishment shall not store TCS food items within the unit until an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less is maintained. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils between uses shall be stored in one of the following manners: on a dry clean surface, in water that is maintained at 135 degrees or more, or in water with sufficient velocity to flush particulates to the drain. Utensils were cleaned and sanitized during the time of inspection and were moved to a dry clean surface. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The kitchen prep unit located was observed unable to maintain an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or less and not in good repair. Establishment is to repair or replace unit. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The door to the toilet room located behind front service line is not self-closing. PIC informed inspector that establishment attempted to fix door to make self-closing and door became cracked. New door is on order and will be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
Recycling unit was observed overflowing. PIC informed that more frequent pickups for disposal required to prevent overflowing refuse accumulations Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
The floors behind the front counter line are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Per PIC, maintenance is scheduled. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Raw eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as fresh fruit and vegetables in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Bottled juices were observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Table top refrigeration unit was observed at an ambient temperature of 49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Bottled juices that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of. Table top refrigeration unit was taken out of service to hold any potentially hazardous beverages and/or foods. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the table top refrigerator and 2 door lowboy refrigerator refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
Water from prep sink was observed draining slowly and overflowing into a bucket. Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The condensate from the dump sink in upstairs service counter is draining into the first basin of the 3 bay sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The door to the toilet room located behind front service line is not self-closing. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
Recycling unit was observed overflowing. PIC informed that more frequent pickups for disposal required to prevent overflowing refuse accumulations. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The floors behind the front counter line are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Per PIC, maintenence is scheduled. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
